vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Structural integrity under wing loading
Thermal management for avionics bay
Electrical connectivity for avionics systems
GPS boom and camera mounting points
Landing gear attachment
Service access for maintenance

verify.value: All required fields are present.

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues in the avionics bay due to insufficient cooling.
risk.mit: Implement enhanced cooling solutions such as heat sinks or fans.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if wing loading exceeds design limits.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough stress analysis and reinforce weak points.

risk.desc: Possibility of electrical connectivity issues affecting data transfer reliability.
risk.mit: Perform rigorous testing and ensure proper insulation and shielding.

risk.desc: Weight distribution may affect flight dynamics if not optimized.
risk.mit: Review and adjust weight distribution based on flight simulation results.

risk.desc: Insufficient service access could hinder maintenance and repairs.
risk.mit: Increase service access cutouts and ensure easy component replacement.

physics_paths.description: Transfers wing loading into fuselage structure.
Data and power transfer between electronics and avionics systems.

integration_opportunities.description: Add additional sensor mounts for future upgrades.
Enhance cooling solutions for avionics bay.
Adjust weight distribution for better flight dynamics.

verification_focus.description: Ensure structural integrity under expected loading conditions.
Verify thermal performance of avionics bay.
Check electrical connectivity and data transfer reliability.

generated_parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

requirements: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible
Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable
Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details
Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers
